newsBriefing: NFL free agency chaos dominated Monday: Miami moved on from Tua Tagovailoa with a post-June 1 release that brings a record dead-cap hit, then dealt safety Minkah Fitzpatrick to the Jets, while the Packers sent pass rusher Rashan Gary to Dallas as teams kept reshaping rosters. Houston kept guard Ed Ingram on a three-year deal, Pittsburgh re-signed Asante Samuel Jr. for one year, and league-wide business tension rose as owners were briefed on increasingly strained labor talks with the referees union ahead of the current CBA deadline. Outside the NFL, Brazilian football erupted when Cruzeiro's state final win over Atletico-MG ended in a mass brawl and 23 red cards, and MMA got a marquee update with Francis Ngannou set to return May 16 against Philipe Lins on Most Valuable Promotions' inaugural MMA card streaming on Netflix.
